---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# NHANES PARTICIPANT ID INTERSECTION MATRIX  —  PER SURVEY CYCLE
# ==============================================================================
#
# DESIGN PHILOSOPHY (addressing professor's question directly)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Pattern matching is flexible but fragile: a regex can silently match an
# unintended target or miss a valid one, with no runtime signal that anything
# went wrong. An explicit whitelist of exact NHANES file codes eliminates
# both failure modes — every accepted value is intentional, and every
# unexpected or missing input produces a loud, visible error.
#
# The remaining sensitivity risk (whitelist entries that are formatted
# differently from what the CDC actually publishes) is addressed by running
# a PRE-FLIGHT MANIFEST AUDIT before any data is downloaded. Every single
# file code in the whitelist is validated against CDC's live nhanesTables()
# output. If a code is wrong, execution stops with a precise error message
# before wasting any download time.
#
# Summary of the defence layers, in order of execution:
#   1. Static whitelist   — exact, intentional, no inference
#   2. Structural checks  — no duplicate codes, no year collisions per dataset
#   3. Manifest audit     — every code confirmed against CDC's live directory
#   4. Download guards    — SEQN presence asserted after each download
#   5. Matrix assertions  — diagonal must equal n_participants, matrix symmetric
#
# Any violation at any layer calls stop() with a diagnostic message.
# Silent failures are not permitted anywhere in this script.
# ==============================================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(nhanesA)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(purrr)
})

# ── Survey cycle mapping (start-year key → NHANES letter suffix) ──────────────
CYCLE_LETTER <- c(
  "2003" = "C", "2005" = "D", "2007" = "E",
  "2009" = "F", "2011" = "G", "2013" = "H",
  "2015" = "I", "2017" = "J"
)
ALL_YEARS <- names(CYCLE_LETTER)


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 1 — THE MASTER WHITELIST
# ==============================================================================
# Rules:
#   • Every entry represents exactly one distinct NHANES dataset as listed in
#     the project's blue-highlighted dataset inventory.
#   • `codes` is a named character vector: year (start-year string) → file code.
#   • A year absent from `codes` means the dataset was NOT collected that cycle.
#     There is no default / fallback logic anywhere downstream.
#   • Entries marked NEEDS_VALIDATION have codes that are uncertain and MUST
#     be confirmed by the pre-flight audit before results are trusted.
#   • Entries that share a file code with another entry in the same year are
#     flagged by the structural checks (Section 2). This is expected for a few
#     datasets that CDC merged into a single file across cycles.
# ==============================================================================

WHITELIST <- list(

  # ── HEAVY METALS (BLOOD) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  # AUDIT FINDING: CDC uses mixed-case "PbCd" (not "PBCD") for cycles D-G.
  # Cycles H-J are uppercase PBCD — confirmed OK in audit, no change needed.
  # Panel expanded in 2011 to add Se and Mn; still the same file series.
  PBCD = list(
    label = "Cadmium, Lead & Total Mercury – Blood (2003-2009)",
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L06BMT_C",  # confirmed OK
      "2005" = "PbCd_D",    # FIXED: was PBCD_D — manifest uses mixed case
      "2007" = "PbCd_E",    # FIXED: was PBCD_E
      "2009" = "PbCd_F"     # FIXED: was PBCD_F
    )
  ),

  PBCD_EXT = list(
    label = "Cadmium, Lead, Hg, Selenium & Manganese – Blood (2011-2017)",
    codes = c(
      "2011" = "PbCd_G",    # FIXED: was PBCD_G — manifest uses mixed case
      "2013" = "PBCD_H",    # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "PBCD_I",    # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "PBCD_J"     # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # ── MERCURY – BLOOD ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  # AUDIT FINDING: Manifest uses "IHg_D" (mixed case) for 2005.
  # From 2007-2009 the file is still "IHg" (not "IHGEM") despite containing
  # the expanded ethyl+methyl panel.  "IHgEM" spelling begins 2011.
  IHG = list(
    label = "Mercury: Inorganic – Blood (2005 only)",
    codes = c(
      "2005" = "IHg_D"      # FIXED: was IHG_D
    )
  ),

  IHGEM = list(
    label = "Mercury: Inorganic, Ethyl & Methyl – Blood (2007-2017)",
    codes = c(
      "2007" = "IHg_E",     # FIXED: was IHGEM_E — CDC still called it IHg in 2007
      "2009" = "IHg_F",     # FIXED: was IHGEM_F — same; IHg naming continues
      "2011" = "IHgEM_G",   # FIXED: was IHGEM_G — IHgEM (mixed) starts 2011
      "2013" = "IHGEM_H",   # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "IHGEM_I",   # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "IHGEM_J"    # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # ── MERCURY – URINE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  UHG = list(
    label = "Mercury: Inorganic – Urine (all cycles)",
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L06UHG_C",  # confirmed OK
      "2005" = "UHG_D",     # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "UHG_E",     # confirmed OK
      "2009" = "UHG_F",     # confirmed OK
      "2011" = "UHG_G",     # confirmed OK
      "2013" = "UHG_H",     # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "UHG_I",     # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "UHG_J"      # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # ── PFAS / POLYFLUOROALKYL ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

  PFC = list(
    label = "Polyfluoroalkyl Chemicals – Serum (2003-2011, PFC series)",
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L24PFC_C",  # confirmed OK
      "2005" = "PFC_D",     # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "PFC_E",     # confirmed OK
      "2009" = "PFC_F",     # confirmed OK
      "2011" = "PFC_G"      # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  PFAS = list(
    label = "PFAS – Serum (2013-2017, renamed from PFC)",
    codes = c(
      "2013" = "PFAS_H",    # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "PFAS_I",    # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "PFAS_J"     # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  SSPFAS = list(
    label = "PFAS – Surplus Serum (2013, 2017)",
    codes = c(
      "2013" = "SSPFAS_H",  # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "SSPFAS_J"   # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # AUDIT FINDING: SSPFC_H not found. Suggestion: SSPFAC_H.
  # Still NEEDS_VALIDATION — the description match is plausible but not certain.
  PFAS_CHILD = list(
    label = "PFAS – Children 3-11 Years Subsample – Serum (2013 only)  [NEEDS_VALIDATION]",
    codes = c(
      "2013" = "SSPFAC_H"   # NEEDS_VALIDATION: FIXED from SSPFC_H per suggestion
    )
  ),

  # ── VOCs – BLOOD ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  VOCWB = list(
    label = "VOCs – Blood (full panel, all cycles)",
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L04VOC_C",  # confirmed OK
      "2005" = "VOCWB_D",   # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "VOCWB_E",   # confirmed OK
      "2009" = "VOCWB_F",   # confirmed OK
      "2011" = "VOCWB_G",   # confirmed OK
      "2013" = "VOCWB_H",   # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "VOCWB_I",   # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "VOCWB_J"    # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # AUDIT FINDING: VOC_TTHM_E not found. Suggestion: VOCMWB_E.
  # 2007 is a separate file; 2009+ this series merged into the main VOCWB files
  # (duplicate codes with VOCWB — logged by structural check, expected).
  VOC_TTHM = list(
    label = "VOCs – Trihalomethanes/MTBE/Nitromethane – Blood  [NEEDS_VALIDATION for 2007]",
    codes = c(
      "2007" = "VOCMWB_E",  # NEEDS_VALIDATION: FIXED from VOC_TTHM_E per suggestion
      "2009" = "VOCWB_F",   # same file as VOCWB — duplicate logged by structural check
      "2011" = "VOCWB_G",   # same file as VOCWB
      "2013" = "VOCWB_H",   # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "VOCWB_I",   # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "VOCWB_J"    # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # ── PHTHALATES – URINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  PHTHTE = list(
    label = "Phthalates & Plasticizers Metabolites – Urine (all cycles)",
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L24PH_C",   # confirmed OK
      "2005" = "PHTHTE_D",  # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "PHTHTE_E",  # confirmed OK
      "2009" = "PHTHTE_F",  # confirmed OK
      "2011" = "PHTHTE_G",  # confirmed OK
      "2013" = "PHTHTE_H",  # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "PHTHTE_I",  # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "PHTHTE_J"   # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # ── PERCHLORATE – URINE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  PERNT = list(
    label = "Perchlorate, Nitrate & Thiocyanate – Urine (2005-2017)",
    codes = c(
      "2005" = "PERNT_D",   # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "PERNT_E",   # confirmed OK
      "2009" = "PERNT_F",   # confirmed OK
      "2011" = "PERNT_G",   # confirmed OK
      "2013" = "PERNT_H",   # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "PERNT_I",   # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "PERNT_J"    # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # ── PESTICIDES – URINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  # AUDIT FINDING: OPD_F (2009) and OPD_H (2013) not found in manifest with
  # zero suggestions returned.  The suggestion engine also drew a blank — no
  # description in either year’s manifest matched “Organophosphate Dialkyl”.
  # This is the strongest signal yet that these two years were either combined
  # into another file or simply not published as a standalone dataset.
  #
  # They are listed in `uncertain_codes` (machine-readable per-code flag) so
  # the audit treats them as NEEDS_VALIDATION rather than hard ERRORs.
  # The confirmed years (2003, 2005, 2007, 2015) are unaffected.
  OPD = list(
    label           = "Organophosphate Insecticides – Dialkyl Phosphate Metabolites – Urine",
    uncertain_codes = c("OPD_F", "OPD_H"),   # not found in CDC manifest; no suggestion
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L26OPD_C",  # confirmed OK
      "2005" = "OPD_D",     # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "OPD_E",     # confirmed OK
      "2009" = "OPD_F",     # NEEDS_VALIDATION — see uncertain_codes above
      # 2011: NOT COLLECTED per inventory
      "2013" = "OPD_H",     # NEEDS_VALIDATION — see uncertain_codes above
      "2015" = "OPD_I"      # confirmed OK
      # 2017: NOT COLLECTED per inventory
    )
  ),

  # AUDIT FINDING: L26PP_C (2003) not found. Top suggestion: L24PP_C.
  # 2005 (PP_D) and 2007 (PP_E) confirmed OK.
  PP = list(
    label = "Pesticides – Current Use / Priority Pesticides – Urine (2003-2007)  [NEEDS_VALIDATION for 2003]",
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L24PP_C",   # NEEDS_VALIDATION: FIXED from L26PP_C per suggestion
      "2005" = "PP_D",      # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "PP_E"       # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # AUDIT FINDINGS:
  #   2003 (L26UPP_C): confirmed OK — no error or warn in audit output.
  #   2005 (PEST_D) not found → suggestion UPP_D (top match for "Environmental Urine")
  #   2007 (PEST_E) not found → suggestion UPP_E
  #   2009 (PEST_F) not found → top suggestion PP_F (same code as PYRET_EARLY)
  #   2011 (PEST_G) not found → top suggestion PP_G
  # Note: if PP_F/PP_G are confirmed correct, these will share a file with
  # PYRET_EARLY in 2009 and overlap with UPHOPM concept in 2011 — that is the
  # expected duplicate scenario (different inventory labels, one CDC file).
  PEST = list(
    label = "Pesticides – Environmental – Urine (2003-2011)  [NEEDS_VALIDATION: 2005-2011]",
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L26UPP_C",  # confirmed OK (was already correct)
      "2005" = "UPP_D",     # NEEDS_VALIDATION: FIXED from PEST_D per suggestion
      "2007" = "UPP_E",     # NEEDS_VALIDATION: FIXED from PEST_E per suggestion
      "2009" = "PP_F",      # NEEDS_VALIDATION: FIXED from PEST_F per suggestion
      "2011" = "PP_G"       # NEEDS_VALIDATION: FIXED from PEST_G per suggestion
    )
  ),

  # AUDIT FINDING: CARB_D and CARB_E raised NO error or warning — both codes
  # are confirmed present in the CDC manifest. NEEDS_VALIDATION tag removed.
  # These are a genuinely separate file from OPD (different analyte panel).
  CARB = list(
    label = "Pesticides – Carbamates & OP Metabolites – Urine (2005-2007)",
    codes = c(
      "2005" = "CARB_D",    # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "CARB_E"     # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # Pyrethroids, Herbicides & OP Metabolites (2007-2009).
  # PP_E and PP_F confirmed OK. PP_E is shared with the PP entry for 2007
  # (duplicate flagged by structural check — expected, one CDC file two labels).
  PYRET_EARLY = list(
    label = "Pyrethroids, Herbicides & OP Metabolites – Urine (2007-2009)",
    codes = c(
      "2007" = "PP_E",      # confirmed OK (duplicate with PP entry — expected)
      "2009" = "PP_F"       # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # Continuation of pyrethroids series under UPHOPM acronym from 2011.
  # UPHOPM_G/H/I confirmed OK.
  UPHOPM = list(
    label = "Pyrethroids, Herbicides & OP Metabolites – Urine (2011-2015, UPHOPM series)",
    codes = c(
      "2011" = "UPHOPM_G",  # confirmed OK
      "2013" = "UPHOPM_H",  # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "UPHOPM_I"   # confirmed OK
      # 2017: NOT COLLECTED per inventory
    )
  ),

  # ── VOC METABOLITES – URINE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  # AUDIT FINDING: UVOC_D (2005), UVOC_E (2007), UVOC_F (2009) NOT in manifest
  # and no plausible suggestions found. These years are removed — the dataset
  # appears to start in 2011. Cycles 2011-2017 confirmed OK.
  UVOC = list(
    label = "VOC Metabolites – Urine (2011-2017; 2005-2009 not published per audit)",
    codes = c(
      # 2005: REMOVED — UVOC_D not in CDC manifest (was also 404 on download)
      # 2007: REMOVED — UVOC_E not in CDC manifest (was also 404 on download)
      # 2009: REMOVED — UVOC_F not in CDC manifest (was also 404 on download)
      "2011" = "UVOC_G",    # confirmed OK
      "2013" = "UVOC_H",    # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "UVOC_I",    # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "UVOC_J"     # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  SSUVOC = list(
    label = "VOC Metabolites – Surplus – Urine (2017 only)",
    codes = c(
      "2017" = "SSUVOC_J"   # confirmed OK
    )
  ),

  # AUDIT FINDING: SSUVCM_I not found in manifest. Top matching suggestion: UVOCS_I.
  # Still NEEDS_VALIDATION — "UVOCS" may stand for "Urine VOC Surplus" but
  # verify against the CDC website before trusting.
  SSUVCM = list(
    label = "VOC Metabolites – Benzene & Methylating Agent Biomarkers – Surplus – Urine (2015 only)  [NEEDS_VALIDATION]",
    codes = c(
      "2015" = "UVOCS_I"    # NEEDS_VALIDATION: FIXED from SSUVCM_I per suggestion
    )
  ),

  # ── PAHs – URINE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  # Duplicate in Excel inventory (rows 48 & 52) — single entry here.
  # L31PAH_C confirmed OK in audit (no error). PAH_D through PAH_G confirmed OK.
  PAH = list(
    label = "Polyaromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) – Urine (2003-2011)",
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L31PAH_C",  # confirmed OK
      "2005" = "PAH_D",     # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "PAH_E",     # confirmed OK
      "2009" = "PAH_F",     # confirmed OK
      "2011" = "PAH_G"      # confirmed OK
      # 2013-2017: NOT COLLECTED per inventory
    )
  ),

  # ── ALBUMIN & CREATININE – URINE ────────────────────────────────────────────

  ALB_CR = list(
    label = "Albumin & Creatinine – Urine (all cycles)",
    codes = c(
      "2003" = "L16_C",     # confirmed OK
      "2005" = "ALB_CR_D",  # confirmed OK
      "2007" = "ALB_CR_E",  # confirmed OK
      "2009" = "ALB_CR_F",  # confirmed OK
      "2011" = "ALB_CR_G",  # confirmed OK
      "2013" = "ALB_CR_H",  # confirmed OK
      "2015" = "ALB_CR_I",  # confirmed OK
      "2017" = "ALB_CR_J"   # confirmed OK
    )
  )
)  # end WHITELIST


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 2 — STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY CHECKS
# Runs entirely on the in-memory WHITELIST before any network calls.
# Any failure here indicates a data-entry error in the whitelist itself.
# ==============================================================================

message("\n[CHECK 1/3] Running structural integrity checks on the whitelist...")

# 2a. All years must be valid start-year keys
for (key in names(WHITELIST)) {
  bad_years <- setdiff(names(WHITELIST[[key]]$codes), ALL_YEARS)
  if (length(bad_years) > 0) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "STRUCTURAL ERROR: Dataset '%s' contains unrecognized year key(s): %s\n  Valid keys: %s",
      key, paste(bad_years, collapse = ", "), paste(ALL_YEARS, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
}

# 2b. All file codes must be non-empty strings
for (key in names(WHITELIST)) {
  for (yr in names(WHITELIST[[key]]$codes)) {
    code <- WHITELIST[[key]]$codes[[yr]]
    if (!is.character(code) || nchar(trimws(code)) == 0) {
      stop(sprintf("STRUCTURAL ERROR: Dataset '%s', year %s has an empty file code.", key, yr))
    }
  }
}

# 2c. Detect duplicate file codes across datasets within the same year.
#     This is NOT automatically an error — a duplicate means two dataset entries
#     in the inventory point to the same NHANES file (e.g., because CDC combined
#     them). It IS important information.  We log it loudly and continue.
all_code_uses <- purrr::imap_dfr(WHITELIST, function(entry, key) {
  purrr::imap_dfr(entry$codes, function(code, yr) {
    data.frame(dataset = key, year = yr, code = code, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  })
})

dup_codes <- all_code_uses %>%
  group_by(year, code) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  arrange(year, code)

if (nrow(dup_codes) > 0) {
  message("\n  [NOTICE] The following file codes appear under more than one dataset",
          " entry in the same year.")
  message("  This means two dataset labels in the inventory share a single CDC file.")
  message("  Their intersection will equal their full sample size (100% overlap).")
  message("  This is EXPECTED for some splits (e.g., VOC_TTHM absorbed into VOCWB).")
  print(as.data.frame(dup_codes))
}

message("  Structural checks passed.")


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3 — PRE-FLIGHT MANIFEST AUDIT
# Downloads CDC's official lab file directory for every cycle and checks that
# every whitelist code is present.  Stops execution if any confirmed code is
# missing.  NEEDS_VALIDATION codes produce a warning instead.
# ==============================================================================

message("\n[CHECK 2/3] Downloading CDC lab manifests for all cycles...")

# Build the set of uncertain codes using TWO complementary mechanisms:
#
#   1. Label-level flag: if the dataset label contains "[NEEDS_VALIDATION]",
#      ALL codes in that entry are treated as uncertain.  Used when an entire
#      dataset's codes are guesses (e.g. PFAS_CHILD, SSUVCM).
#
#   2. Code-level flag: the `uncertain_codes` field lists individual codes
#      within an otherwise-confirmed entry that could not be validated.
#      This is more precise — it avoids marking confirmed sibling codes as
#      uncertain just because one year is ambiguous (e.g. OPD_F / OPD_H
#      within the OPD entry whose 2003/2005/2007/2015 codes are all confirmed).
#
# Both are unioned into `needs_validation_codes`.  A code in this set that
# fails the manifest lookup emits [WARN] instead of [ERROR].

needs_validation_codes <- character(0)
for (key in names(WHITELIST)) {
  entry <- WHITELIST[[key]]

  # Mechanism 1 — label-level: all codes for this entry are uncertain.
  # Exception: if a code appears in another whitelist entry for the same year
  # whose label does NOT contain NEEDS_VALIDATION, it is a shared/merged file
  # that has already been confirmed via that sibling entry.  Including it in
  # uncertain_codes would produce a misleading [CASE] notice and inflate the
  # uncertain count.  We resolve this by computing the set of codes that are
  # confirmed in at least one OTHER entry before including them.
  if (grepl("NEEDS_VALIDATION", entry$label)) {
    entry_codes <- unname(entry$codes)
    # Find all codes that appear in a confirmed (non-NEEDS_VALIDATION) entry
    confirmed_elsewhere <- character(0)
    for (other_key in names(WHITELIST)) {
      if (other_key == key) next
      if (!grepl("NEEDS_VALIDATION", WHITELIST[[other_key]]$label) &&
          is.null(WHITELIST[[other_key]]$uncertain_codes)) {
        confirmed_elsewhere <- c(confirmed_elsewhere,
                                  unname(WHITELIST[[other_key]]$codes))
      }
    }
    confirmed_elsewhere <- unique(confirmed_elsewhere)
    # Only mark as uncertain those codes NOT confirmed via a sibling entry
    truly_uncertain <- setdiff(entry_codes, confirmed_elsewhere)
    needs_validation_codes <- c(needs_validation_codes, truly_uncertain)
  }

  # Mechanism 2 — code-level: specific codes listed in uncertain_codes field
  if (!is.null(entry$uncertain_codes)) {
    if (!is.character(entry$uncertain_codes)) {
      stop(sprintf(
        "STRUCTURAL ERROR: '%s'$uncertain_codes must be a character vector, got %s",
        key, class(entry$uncertain_codes)
      ))
    }
    # Assert every listed uncertain code actually exists in this entry's codes
    bad <- setdiff(entry$uncertain_codes, unname(entry$codes))
    if (length(bad) > 0) {
      stop(sprintf(
        "STRUCTURAL ERROR: '%s'$uncertain_codes references code(s) not in $codes: %s",
        key, paste(bad, collapse = ", ")
      ))
    }
    needs_validation_codes <- c(needs_validation_codes, entry$uncertain_codes)
  }
}
needs_validation_codes <- unique(needs_validation_codes)
message(sprintf("  Uncertain codes registered: %d  (%s)",
                length(needs_validation_codes),
                paste(needs_validation_codes, collapse = ", ")))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MANIFEST COLUMN RESOLUTION
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# nhanesA returns different raw column names depending on the cycle year and
# package version.  After stripping punctuation and lowercasing, the known
# variants are:
#
#   Newer cycles (2005+):  "docfile"       → short file code  (e.g. PBCD_D)
#                          "datafilename"  → human description
#
#   Older cycles (2003):   "datafilename"  → short file code  (e.g. L06BMT_C)
#                          "datafiledescription" → human description
#
# Priority order is therefore:  docfile > datafilecode > filename > datafilename
# For newer years "docfile" wins immediately.
# For 2003 only "datafilename" survives to become the fallback — and for that
# cycle it genuinely contains the file codes.
#
# A per-cycle diagnostic block is printed immediately after each download so
# that any future column-name change is immediately visible without needing to
# re-run everything.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CANDIDATE_CODE_COLS <- c("docfile", "datafilecode", "filename", "datafilename")

# Download manifests (one per cycle year = one CDC call per year)
cdc_manifests      <- list()
manifest_code_cols <- character(0)   # record which column was chosen per year

for (yr in ALL_YEARS) {
  yr_int <- as.integer(yr)
  message("  Fetching manifest for cycle starting ", yr, "...")
  Sys.sleep(0.3)
  manifest_raw <- tryCatch(
    nhanesTables("LAB", yr_int),
    error = function(e) {
      stop(sprintf(
        "MANIFEST FETCH FAILED for year %s: %s\n  Cannot validate whitelist without CDC manifest.",
        yr, e$message
      ))
    }
  )

  # Normalise column names (nhanesA changes them between versions)
  names(manifest_raw) <- tolower(gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "", names(manifest_raw)))

  # Identify which code column this cycle exposes, and print it loudly so any
  # future column-name change surfaces immediately.
  resolved_col <- intersect(CANDIDATE_CODE_COLS, names(manifest_raw))
  if (length(resolved_col) == 0) {
    stop(sprintf(paste0(
      "MANIFEST STRUCTURE ERROR: Cannot find a file-code column in the %s manifest.\n",
      "  Columns present after normalisation: %s\n",
      "  Expected one of: %s\n",
      "  This means nhanesA changed its output format. Add the new column name to\n",
      "  CANDIDATE_CODE_COLS at the top of this block and re-run."
    ), yr,
       paste(names(manifest_raw), collapse = ", "),
       paste(CANDIDATE_CODE_COLS,  collapse = ", ")))
  }
  resolved_col <- resolved_col[1]
  manifest_code_cols[yr] <- resolved_col

  # Diagnostic: show the column chosen and a sample of its values
  sample_vals <- head(manifest_raw[[resolved_col]], 4)
  message(sprintf(
    "    [MANIFEST DIAG %s] columns: (%s)  |  code col chosen: '%s'  |  sample values: %s",
    yr,
    paste(names(manifest_raw), collapse = ", "),
    resolved_col,
    paste(sample_vals, collapse = ", ")
  ))

  cdc_manifests[[yr]] <- manifest_raw
}

message("\n  Column resolution summary (year → column used for file-code lookup):")
for (yr in ALL_YEARS) {
  message(sprintf("    %s → '%s'", yr, manifest_code_cols[yr]))
}

# Run validation: every whitelist code must appear in the manifest for its year
message("\n  Validating whitelist codes against CDC manifests...")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DESCRIPTION-COLUMN RESOLUTION
# When a code fails validation we need to search the manifest by description
# to suggest the correct code.  The description column also varies by year:
#
#   Newer cycles:  "datafilename"      → human description
#   Older (2003):  "datafiledescription" → human description
#                  (datafilename holds the code, so desc is the other column)
#
# We resolve once per year and store alongside the code column.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CANDIDATE_DESC_COLS <- c("datafilename", "datafiledescription", "description",
                          "docfile")  # docfile last — only reached if nothing else exists

manifest_desc_cols <- character(0)

for (yr in ALL_YEARS) {
  manifest <- cdc_manifests[[yr]]
  code_col <- manifest_code_cols[[yr]]
  # Description column = highest-priority candidate that is NOT the code column
  desc_candidates <- setdiff(CANDIDATE_DESC_COLS, code_col)
  resolved_desc   <- intersect(desc_candidates, names(manifest))
  if (length(resolved_desc) == 0) {
    # No separate description column — fall back to code col (suggestions will
    # be less readable but still functional)
    manifest_desc_cols[yr] <- code_col
    message(sprintf("  [DIAG] %s: no separate description column found; using '%s' for suggestions",
                    yr, code_col))
  } else {
    manifest_desc_cols[yr] <- resolved_desc[1]
  }
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUGGESTION ENGINE
# For a failed code, derive keyword terms from the dataset label, search the
# manifest description column for rows containing ALL terms, and return the
# top-5 matching file codes.  This eliminates manual hunting.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
suggest_correct_code <- function(failed_code, dataset_key, year, n_suggest = 5) {
  manifest  <- cdc_manifests[[year]]
  code_col  <- manifest_code_cols[[year]]
  desc_col  <- manifest_desc_cols[[year]]

  # Build keywords from the dataset label: strip punctuation, split on spaces,
  # keep tokens longer than 3 chars, take first 4 (enough to be specific,
  # not so many that a minor label difference breaks the search).
  raw_label <- WHITELIST[[dataset_key]]$label
  clean     <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9 ]", " ", raw_label)
  tokens    <- strsplit(clean, "\\s+")[[1]]
  keywords  <- tokens[nchar(tokens) > 3][1:min(4, sum(nchar(tokens) > 3))]

  # Search description column: row must match at least ONE keyword (OR logic
  # gives more results; we rank by how many keywords match).
  if (length(keywords) == 0 || is.na(keywords[1])) {
    return(character(0))
  }
  descriptions <- manifest[[desc_col]]
  keyword_hits <- sapply(keywords, function(kw) {
    grepl(kw, descriptions, ignore.case = TRUE)
  })
  if (is.vector(keyword_hits)) keyword_hits <- matrix(keyword_hits, ncol = 1)
  match_count <- rowSums(keyword_hits)

  candidates <- manifest[match_count > 0, , drop = FALSE]
  candidates$match_score <- match_count[match_count > 0]
  candidates <- candidates[order(-candidates$match_score), ]

  if (nrow(candidates) == 0) return(character(0))

  head(candidates[[code_col]], n_suggest)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MAIN VALIDATION LOOP
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
validation_results <- list()
audit_has_errors   <- FALSE
correction_rows    <- list()   # accumulates suggestions for failed codes

for (key in names(WHITELIST)) {
  entry <- WHITELIST[[key]]
  for (yr in names(entry$codes)) {
    code     <- entry$codes[[yr]]
    manifest <- cdc_manifests[[yr]]
    code_col <- manifest_code_cols[[yr]]

    # Case-insensitive lookup — CDC manifest capitalisation is inconsistent
    # across cycles (e.g. "PbCd_D" in 2005, "PBCD_H" in 2013, "IHg_E" in 2007).
    # We fold to uppercase on both sides so a casing discrepancy does not mask
    # a genuinely correct code.  nhanesA normalises the code when constructing
    # the XPT download URL, so case only matters for this string comparison.
    manifest_upper <- toupper(manifest[[code_col]])
    found_exact    <- code %in% manifest[[code_col]]
    found          <- toupper(code) %in% manifest_upper

    # Emit a [CASE] notice when the match required case-folding so the
    # whitelist can be updated to use CDC's exact capitalisation.
    if (found && !found_exact) {
      actual_code <- manifest[[code_col]][manifest_upper == toupper(code)][1]
      message(sprintf(
        "  [CASE]   '%s' (%s / %s) matched as '%s' — update whitelist to CDC casing.",
        code, key, yr, actual_code
      ))
    }

    is_uncertain <- code %in% needs_validation_codes

    status <- if (found) "OK" else if (is_uncertain) "NEEDS_VALIDATION_MISSING" else "ERROR_MISSING"

    if (status == "ERROR_MISSING") {
      audit_has_errors <- TRUE
      suggestions <- suggest_correct_code(code, key, yr)
      sugg_str    <- if (length(suggestions) > 0) paste(suggestions, collapse = " | ") else "(no matches found)"
      message(sprintf(
        "  [ERROR]  '%s' (%s / %s) — NOT FOUND in CDC manifest for %s\n           Suggestions: %s",
        code, key, yr, yr, sugg_str
      ))
      correction_rows[[length(correction_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        dataset     = key,
        year        = yr,
        bad_code    = code,
        suggestions = sugg_str,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    } else if (status == "NEEDS_VALIDATION_MISSING") {
      suggestions <- suggest_correct_code(code, key, yr)
      sugg_str    <- if (length(suggestions) > 0) paste(suggestions, collapse = " | ") else "(no matches found)"
      message(sprintf(
        "  [WARN]   '%s' (%s / %s) — not found (marked NEEDS_VALIDATION)\n           Suggestions: %s",
        code, key, yr, sugg_str
      ))
      correction_rows[[length(correction_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        dataset     = key,
        year        = yr,
        bad_code    = code,
        suggestions = sugg_str,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }

    validation_results[[length(validation_results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      dataset = key, year = yr, code = code,
      status = status, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
}

audit_df <- bind_rows(validation_results)
n_ok   <- sum(audit_df$status == "OK")
n_warn <- sum(audit_df$status == "NEEDS_VALIDATION_MISSING")
n_err  <- sum(audit_df$status == "ERROR_MISSING")

message(sprintf("\n  Audit complete: %d OK  |  %d NEEDS_VALIDATION not found  |  %d errors",
                n_ok, n_warn, n_err))

# Always save the full audit log + correction suggestions regardless of outcome
write.csv(audit_df, "nhanes_audit_log.csv", row.names = FALSE)
message("  Full audit log saved → nhanes_audit_log.csv")

if (length(correction_rows) > 0) {
  corrections_df <- bind_rows(correction_rows)
  write.csv(corrections_df, "nhanes_audit_corrections_needed.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  message("  Suggested corrections saved → nhanes_audit_corrections_needed.csv")
  message("  Open that file, verify each suggestion against the CDC website,")
  message("  then update the corresponding entry in the WHITELIST and re-run.")
}

# Also dump the full manifest for every year so you can search manually if
# the suggestion engine misses something.
for (yr in ALL_YEARS) {
  fname <- sprintf("nhanes_manifest_%s.csv", yr)
  write.csv(cdc_manifests[[yr]], fname, row.names = FALSE)
}
message("  Full manifests saved → nhanes_manifest_<year>.csv  (one per cycle)")

if (audit_has_errors) {
  stop(paste0(
    "\n================================================================================\n",
    "  PRE-FLIGHT AUDIT FAILED — ", n_err, " confirmed code(s) not found in CDC manifest\n",
    "\n",
    "  NEXT STEPS:\n",
    "  1. Open nhanes_audit_corrections_needed.csv\n",
    "     Each row shows the bad code and the top 5 description-matched suggestions.\n",
    "  2. Cross-check suggestions against the matching nhanes_manifest_<year>.csv\n",
    "  3. Update the WHITELIST entries in this script with the correct codes.\n",
    "  4. Re-run.  The audit will confirm the fix before any data is downloaded.\n",
    "================================================================================\n"
  ))
}

# Identify which codes are confirmed-good for downstream use
confirmed_codes <- audit_df %>% filter(status == "OK") %>%
  select(dataset, year, code)

message("\n[CHECK 2/3] Manifest audit passed for all confirmed codes.")
if (n_warn > 0) {
  message("  NOTE: ", n_warn, " NEEDS_VALIDATION code(s) were not found and will be SKIPPED.",
          "\n  Their dataset entries will have NA in the intersection matrix for those years.")
}


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 4 — DOWNLOAD PARTICIPANT IDs (SEQN) PER YEAR
# Only downloads files that passed manifest validation.
# Assertions fire loudly on any unexpected result.
# ==============================================================================

message("\n[CHECK 3/3] Beginning SEQN downloads for confirmed codes only...")

# Returns a validated integer vector of SEQNs, or stops loudly.
# download_seqn returns a validated integer vector of SEQNs, or NULL if the
# XPT file is unavailable at CDC (returns NULL from nhanes() with a message).
# Unavailability is treated as a SKIP — the dataset gets NA in the matrix for
# that year, the same as if it was never collected.  A hard stop() is still
# used for genuine errors (network failure, missing SEQN column, duplicates).
skipped_downloads <- character(0)   # accumulates "CODE (KEY / YR)" strings

download_seqn <- function(file_code, dataset_key, year) {
  Sys.sleep(0.4)  # polite throttle
  message("    Downloading: ", file_code, "  (", dataset_key, " / ", year, ")")

  df <- tryCatch(
    nhanes(file_code),
    error = function(e) {
      stop(sprintf(
        "DOWNLOAD FAILED: %s (%s / %s)\n  Error: %s",
        file_code, dataset_key, year, e$message
      ))
    }
  )

  # NULL means CDC returned a "not available" message — skip gracefully
  if (is.null(df)) {
    skipped_downloads <<- c(skipped_downloads,
      sprintf("%s (%s / %s)", file_code, dataset_key, year))
    message(sprintf(
      "    [SKIP] %s (%s / %s) — CDC returned NULL (file not available). Will be NA in matrix.",
      file_code, dataset_key, year
    ))
    return(NULL)
  }
  if (!"SEQN" %in% names(df)) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "SEQN COLUMN MISSING: %s (%s / %s)\n  Columns present: %s",
      file_code, dataset_key, year, paste(names(df), collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
  ids <- as.integer(df$SEQN)
  if (any(is.na(ids))) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "NA VALUES IN SEQN: %s (%s / %s) — %d NA(s) found out of %d rows",
      file_code, dataset_key, year, sum(is.na(ids)), nrow(df)
    ))
  }
  if (length(ids) == 0) {
    stop(sprintf("SEQN VECTOR IS EMPTY: %s (%s / %s)", file_code, dataset_key, year))
  }
  if (length(unique(ids)) != length(ids)) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "DUPLICATE SEQNs DETECTED: %s (%s / %s) — %d duplicate ID(s)",
      file_code, dataset_key, year,
      length(ids) - length(unique(ids))
    ))
  }
  ids
}

# Build: id_store[[year]][[dataset_key]] = integer vector of SEQNs (or NULL)
id_store <- list()

for (yr in ALL_YEARS) {
  message("\n  === Cycle ", yr, " ===")
  id_store[[yr]] <- list()

  for (key in names(WHITELIST)) {
    confirmed_row <- confirmed_codes %>%
      filter(dataset == key, year == yr)

    if (nrow(confirmed_row) == 0) {
      id_store[[yr]][[key]] <- NULL
      next
    }

    if (nrow(confirmed_row) > 1) {
      stop(sprintf(
        "ASSERTION FAILED: More than one confirmed code for dataset '%s' in year %s: %s",
        key, yr, paste(confirmed_row$code, collapse = ", ")
      ))
    }

    result <- download_seqn(confirmed_row$code, key, yr)
    id_store[[yr]][[key]] <- result   # NULL on skip, integer vector on success
  }
}

message("\nAll downloads complete.")
if (length(skipped_downloads) > 0) {
  message(sprintf(
    "  [SKIP SUMMARY] %d file(s) were unavailable at CDC and excluded from matrices:",
    length(skipped_downloads)
  ))
  for (s in skipped_downloads) message("    • ", s)
}


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 5 — BUILD INTERSECTION MATRICES (ONE PER CYCLE)
# ==============================================================================

# Widen console so matrices don't wrap
options(width = 200)

# Helper: print an integer matrix compactly.
# Each cell is right-justified to the width of the widest number in its column.
# NAs are printed as "." to avoid noise.
print_matrix_compact <- function(mat) {
  # Convert to character, replacing NA with "."
  char_mat <- matrix(
    ifelse(is.na(mat), ".", formatC(mat, format = "d", big.mark = ",")),
    nrow = nrow(mat),
    dimnames = dimnames(mat)
  )
  # Column widths: max of header width and cell width
  col_w <- pmax(nchar(colnames(char_mat)),
                apply(char_mat, 2, function(x) max(nchar(x))))
  row_w <- max(nchar(rownames(char_mat)))   # row-label width

  # Header line
  header <- paste0(
    sprintf("%-*s", row_w, ""),
    paste(sprintf("%*s", col_w, colnames(char_mat)), collapse = "  ")
  )
  cat(header, "\n")
  # Separator
  cat(strrep("-", nchar(header)), "\n")
  # Data rows
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(char_mat))) {
    row_line <- paste0(
      sprintf("%-*s", row_w, rownames(char_mat)[i]),
      paste(sprintf("%*s", col_w, char_mat[i, ]), collapse = "  ")
    )
    cat(row_line, "\n")
  }
}

message("\nBuilding per-cycle intersection matrices...")

dataset_keys <- names(WHITELIST)
n_datasets   <- length(dataset_keys)

per_year_matrices <- list()   # integer matrices (NA where either dataset absent)
long_rows         <- list()

for (yr in ALL_YEARS) {
  message("  Building matrix for ", yr, "...")

  # Which datasets have confirmed data this year?
  active_keys <- dataset_keys[
    sapply(dataset_keys, function(k) !is.null(id_store[[yr]][[k]]))
  ]

  # Full N x N matrix; NA where one or both datasets not collected
  mat <- matrix(NA_integer_, nrow = n_datasets, ncol = n_datasets,
                dimnames = list(dataset_keys, dataset_keys))

  for (i in seq_len(n_datasets)) {
    for (j in seq_len(n_datasets)) {
      ki <- dataset_keys[i]
      kj <- dataset_keys[j]
      ids_i <- id_store[[yr]][[ki]]
      ids_j <- id_store[[yr]][[kj]]

      if (!is.null(ids_i) && !is.null(ids_j)) {
        overlap <- length(intersect(ids_i, ids_j))

        # Assertion: diagonal must equal the full sample size
        if (i == j && overlap != length(ids_i)) {
          stop(sprintf(
            "DIAGONAL ASSERTION FAILED: %s / %s — intersection(%d) != n_ids(%d)",
            ki, yr, overlap, length(ids_i)
          ))
        }
        mat[i, j] <- overlap
      }
    }
  }

  # Symmetry assertion (intersection is commutative)
  active_idx <- which(dataset_keys %in% active_keys)
  sub <- mat[active_idx, active_idx, drop = FALSE]
  if (!isSymmetric(sub, na.rm = FALSE)) {
    stop(sprintf("SYMMETRY ASSERTION FAILED: Matrix for year %s is not symmetric.", yr))
  }

  per_year_matrices[[yr]] <- mat

  # Print the active sub-matrix using file codes as labels, formatted to fit
  # on one screen without wrapping.
  if (length(active_keys) > 1) {
    codes_this_yr <- sapply(active_keys, function(k) {
      code <- WHITELIST[[k]]$codes[yr]
      if (is.na(code)) k else unname(code)
    })
    sub_coded <- sub
    rownames(sub_coded) <- codes_this_yr
    colnames(sub_coded) <- codes_this_yr
    cat(sprintf("\n══ Intersection Matrix: %s (%d datasets) ══\n", yr, length(active_keys)))
    print_matrix_compact(sub_coded)
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("\n  %s: only %d dataset — no pairs.\n", yr, length(active_keys)))
  }

  # Accumulate long-format rows (upper triangle + diagonal)
  for (i in seq_len(n_datasets)) {
    for (j in i:n_datasets) {
      ki <- dataset_keys[i]
      kj <- dataset_keys[j]
      long_rows[[length(long_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        year      = yr,
        dataset_a = ki,
        label_a   = WHITELIST[[ki]]$label,
        dataset_b = kj,
        label_b   = WHITELIST[[kj]]$label,
        n_overlap = mat[i, j],
        n_a       = if (!is.null(id_store[[yr]][[ki]])) length(id_store[[yr]][[ki]]) else NA_integer_,
        n_b       = if (!is.null(id_store[[yr]][[kj]])) length(id_store[[yr]][[kj]]) else NA_integer_,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }
}


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 6 — OVERALL MATRIX + KEY + SUMMARY  (no files written)
# ==============================================================================

# ── 6a. Combined all-years matrix ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# For each pair (i,j), sum overlaps across all cycles in which BOTH datasets
# were collected.  This gives a single N×N view across 2003-2017.
cat("\n══ Overall Intersection Matrix: All Cycles 2003-2017 (sum of shared participants) ══\n")

combined <- matrix(0L, nrow = n_datasets, ncol = n_datasets,
                   dimnames = list(dataset_keys, dataset_keys))
count_mat <- matrix(0L, nrow = n_datasets, ncol = n_datasets,
                    dimnames = list(dataset_keys, dataset_keys))

for (yr in ALL_YEARS) {
  mat <- per_year_matrices[[yr]]
  for (i in seq_len(n_datasets)) {
    for (j in seq_len(n_datasets)) {
      if (!is.na(mat[i, j])) {
        combined[i, j]  <- combined[i, j]  + mat[i, j]
        count_mat[i, j] <- count_mat[i, j] + 1L
      }
    }
  }
}

# Only show rows/cols where the dataset appeared in at least one year
active_any <- dataset_keys[apply(count_mat, 1, function(x) any(x > 0))]
active_idx <- which(dataset_keys %in% active_any)
comb_sub   <- combined[active_idx, active_idx, drop = FALSE]

# Row/col labels: use the dataset key (e.g. PBCD, ALB_CR) — cleaner than a
# year-specific file code for the combined matrix
rownames(comb_sub) <- active_any
colnames(comb_sub) <- active_any
print_matrix_compact(comb_sub)

# ── 6b. Dataset key ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n══ Dataset Key ══\n")

key_rows <- purrr::imap_dfr(WHITELIST, function(entry, key) {
  label <- entry$label
  specimen <- dplyr::case_when(
    grepl("Blood|Serum", label, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Blood/Serum",
    grepl("Urine",       label, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Urine",
    TRUE                                             ~ "Other"
  )
  # Full description: strip trailing parenthetical year range, NEEDS_VALIDATION
  # tag, and the em-dash specimen suffix so the cell is readable
  desc <- gsub("\\s*\\(.*",  "", label)
  desc <- gsub("\\s*\\[.*",  "", desc)
  desc <- gsub("\\s*\u2013.*", "", desc)
  desc <- trimws(desc)
  yrs  <- paste(sort(names(entry$codes)), collapse = " ")
  data.frame(Acronym     = key,
             Specimen    = specimen,
             Description = desc,
             Years       = yrs,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
})

col_w <- c(
  max(nchar(key_rows$Acronym),      nchar("Acronym")),
  max(nchar(key_rows$Specimen),     nchar("Specimen")),
  max(nchar(key_rows$Description),  nchar("Description")),
  max(nchar(key_rows$Years),        nchar("Years"))
)
fmt <- paste0("  %-*s  %-*s  %-*s  %s")
header <- sprintf(fmt,
  col_w[1], "Acronym",
  col_w[2], "Specimen",
  col_w[3], "Description",
  "Years")
sep_line <- paste0("  ", strrep("-", nchar(header) - 2))
cat(header, "\n", sep_line, "\n", sep = "")
for (i in seq_len(nrow(key_rows))) {
  cat(sprintf(fmt,
    col_w[1], key_rows$Acronym[i],
    col_w[2], key_rows$Specimen[i],
    col_w[3], key_rows$Description[i],
    key_rows$Years[i]), "\n")
}

# ── 6c. Top-20 pairs across all cycles ────────────────────────────────────────
overlap_long <- bind_rows(long_rows) %>%
  filter(!is.na(n_overlap), dataset_a != dataset_b) %>%
  arrange(year, desc(n_overlap))

pair_summary <- overlap_long %>%
  group_by(dataset_a, dataset_b) %>%
  summarise(
    n_years       = n(),
    years         = paste(sort(unique(year)), collapse = " "),
    total_overlap = sum(n_overlap, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_yr_n      = max(n_overlap, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_overlap))

cat("\n══ Top 20 Dataset Pairs by Total Overlap (all cycles combined) ══\n")
top20 <- head(pair_summary, 20)
if (nrow(top20) == 0) {
  cat("  (no pairs found)\n")
} else {
  print(top20, row.names = FALSE)
}

cat("\nDone. All matrices printed above. No files written.\n")
```
